content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array AAA of length NNN, and an **odd** integer MMM, let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the number of ordered pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and Ai+Aj=MA_i + A_j = MAi​+Aj​=M.

You are also given a parameter KKK, and want to make f(A)≥Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)≥K. Find the minimum number of array replacements you need to perform to make f(A)≥Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)≥K.

Formally, in one replacement, you choose an index XXX, an integer YYY and change AXA_XAX​ to YYY.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, MMM and KKK - the number of elements in the array, and the parameters MMM and KKK
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of replacements needed. If it is impossible to make f(A)≥Kf(A) \ge Kf(A)≥K no matter what, output −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: The sum of N3N^3N3 over all test cases does not exceed 10810^8108.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Set A2=4A_2 = 4A2​=4, obtaining [1,4,1,4][1, 4, 1, 4][1,4,1,4] which has 444 pairs of elements that sum up to M=5M = 5M=5.
Test case 222: There are already two pairs of elements summing up to M=5M = 5M=5, and since K=2K = 2K=2 no moves are needed.
Test case 444: It's impossible to have ≥2\geq 2≥2 pairs of elements sum up to M=3M = 3M=3 no matter what is done.
